Subject: Re: [opensuse] See packages that were updated on a certain day?
Body: -----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE----- Hash: SHA1 The Sunday 2008-02-10 at 15:15 -0500, Chris Arnold wrote: > Using 10.1 and would like to view all packages that were updated on Sat the 9th. Is there any way to do this? rpm -q -a --queryformat "%{INSTALLTIME}\\t%{INSTALLTIME:day} \\ %{BUILDTIME:day} %-30{NAME}\\t%15{VERSION}-%-7{RELEASE}\\t%{arch} \\ %25{PACKAGER}\\n" | sort | cut --fields="2-" | less -S The first column of the output is the date. Just search or grep. - -- Cheers, Carlos E. R. -----BEGIN PGP SIGNATURE----- Version: GnuPG v2.0.4-svn0 (GNU/Linux) iD8DBQFHr17TtTMYHG2NR9URAsVyAJ45rLooueheVoTwf2IIQb08Y8kfWwCfTJRG RiJ2BEnYRCFizz2HdbdcWzo= =iMio -----END PGP SIGNATURE-----

Subject: community@email-ws.withings.com
Body: Hello, We're pleased to inform you that a new Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) method has been successfully activated on your account. This extra layer of security helps ensure that you're the only person who can access your account, even if someone knows your password or can access your email account. Date of change: 2025-02-13 ΎδκΎ╕Π Your Account's Security Just Leveled Up! By enabling MFA, you've taken a significant step towards enhancing the safety and integrity of your account. However, it's essential to be prepared and know how to use your Recovery Method. ΎΦΣ Recovery Method: In the event you lose access to your MFA, recovery codes can provide backup access to your account. We cannot stress enough how important it is to store your recovery codes in a safe, accessible location. ΎΦΩ Retrieve Your Recovery Codes ΎΤυ Need Help or Have Questions? If you're new to MFA or have any questions about its usage, we've got you covered. Visit our dedicated FAQ to learn more about how it works and why it's important. ΎΦΩ Visit our MFA FAQ έζΩ Didn't Initiate This Action? If you did not activate MFA or believe your account may be compromised, please contact our support team immediately. Your security is our top priority. Regards, Withings

Subject: Re: [zzzzteana] Not a materialisation, but a transfiguration
Body:
On Tue, 27 Aug 2002 MICGRANGER@aol.com wrote:

> Concerning this mail, what is your intention?

When posting to this list excerpts from books I've just read, I usually
refrain from adding any comments, letting the listmembers interpret them
as they see fit.

But since you asked....

I chose to post this text simply because I thought it was a particularly
risible example of Doyle's invincible faith and his refusal to accept the
fucking obvious.

bc

Subject: Re: svn commit: r619753 - in /spamassassin/trunk: lib/Mail/SpamAssassin/PerMsgStatus.pm lib/Mail/SpamAssassin/Util/RegistrarBoundaries.pm t/uri_text.t
Body: I'm happy enough with that. btw this discussion should be on a bug... Sidney Markowitz writes: > Michael Parker wrote, On 8/2/08 4:31 PM: > > Would anyone object to removing .so from this list? > > After reading the Wikipedia entries for the TLDs that they list as unused, I would be in > favor of removing all of them > > bv gb pm sj so um yt > > If spammers can't use them as domains, then all including them could do would be to > generate false positives. > > The three listed as "phaseout", su tp yu, on the other hand, I would keep, because it > appears from the Wikipedia articles that they are in active use either during a transition > period or because their phaseout is being resisted. > > If we can reach consensus on this, I'll make the changes to the code I committed. > > -- sidney

Subject: Halloween Raffle for all employees!
Body: Halloween Raffle Newsletter Halloween Raffle We are running a Halloween raffle this year which will have several prizes that an employee will be randomly selected to win, prizes including: 100$ AMAZON GIFT CARD The top prize for this raffle is a 100$ Amazon Gift card. Start your Christmas shopping early or treat yourself to something this Autumn. 4 x 25$ Amazon Gift Cards Smaller consolation Gift cards with Amazon again giving you variety and option to treat yourself or your loved ones. Redeemable across all of the Amazon marketplace including Books, Films, TV, Music & Games, Home Garden Pets and DIY, Toys (Children and Baby) Sports & Outdoors, Health & Beauty and many many more. 5 x 12-Month Spotify Subscription A Paid 12 month subscription to Spotify granting you access to millions of Songs and other contents from your faviorute artists. The latest songs, Albums, Podcasts and video streaming will all be at your fingertips via Desktop, Mobile and Web applications. Time to enter! Now you have seen what is on offer, go ahead and enter yourself with the below raffle entry button. Simply enter the details and you will be automatically enrolled. Entries will close on 26th October @ 10am, with Prize draw done by end of business day. Prizes will be allocated on draw and winners informed 27th with a view to deliver prizes before Halloween.
